issues.title: The Default System Prompt is not the first element in the messages array
issues.body: Please do a quick search on GitHub issues first, there might be already a duplicate issue for the one you are about to create. If the bug is trivial, just go ahead and create the issue. Otherwise, please take a few moments and fill in the following sections: **Bug description** When we use the chatClient class with "defaultSystem" and "MessageChatMemoryAdvisor", when making the call to OpenAI with a message history, the first element of the array is not of role "system" **Environment** Spring AI: 1.0.0-M5 Java version: 21.0.5-zulu **Steps to reproduce** I created a chatClient linked to a "defaultSystem" and with the "MessageChatMemoryAdvisor", and checked the order of the messages sent to OpenAI. **Expected behavior** The default system message will always be the first message in the array sent to OpenAI. **Minimal Complete Reproducible example** Please provide a failing test or a minimal complete verifiable example that reproduces the issue. Bug reports that are reproducible will take priority in resolution over reports that are not reproducible.

comments.body: Thanks for reporting this issue. I would recommend addressing this together with https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/issues/873, making sure to implement a consistent and predictable strategy for placing system messages across different use cases.
This has been fixed.  Please reopen if there is still an issue.

issues.title: Switch to updated function calling APIs to use new tool calling APIs in  chat models
issues.body:
Updates needed for 

- [x] OpenAI
- [x] Azure OpenAI
- [x] Mistral
- [x] Bedrock Converse
- [x] Anthropic
- [x] Google Vertex
- [x] Ollama

comments.body: Bedrock, Anthropic and Gemini are also migrated. This task can be closed now.

issues.title: Regression on class OpenAiApi Builder or Costructor requiring api key
issues.body:
If the api key is not set or empty it fail with exception "API key value must not be null or empty"

However some openAI API-compatible servers (for example openvino server) to deploy local models do not require an api key. 

So this is a breaking change on spring-ai-openai 1.0.0-SNAPSHOT

comments.body: @fedecompa did you found a workaround for that?

I set a default unused key: 

.apiKey(new SimpleApiKey("openvino"))
